steps.title: Convert percentage decrease to decay factor
steps.content: If enrollment is 2% less each year, it retains 100% - 2% = 98% of the prior year's value. The annual multiplier is $0.98$.

steps.title: Construct the exponential model
steps.content:
Starting with 3,000 students in 2015, multiply by 0.98 each year. After $x$ years:
$$f(x) = 3{,}000 \times (0.98)^x$$
This matches choice D — D is correct.
